Description: Remix routing works best when layout and data boundaries follow the route tree
Do: Use nested route modules and Outlet

Description: Keep initial data on the server and colocated with the route
Form submissions and mutations belong in actions
Do: Use Form or fetcher with action

Description: Remix forms should work before extra JS polish
Do: Build around Form and native navigation
Don't: Depend on client-only handlers for core flows
Code Bad: Entire flow hidden behind JS only

Description: Surface loading and submitting states from Remix primitives

Don't: Wrap every component in ad-hoc try-catch

Description: Server actions define truth for form input
Use headers and response helpers intentionally
Do: Set cache headers on loader responses

Description: Use shouldRevalidate only when the default is too noisy
Do: Keep defaults unless a real problem exists

Description: Remix separates code by execution environment
Do: Centralize server-only modules and env access
Don't: Import server code into browser bundles

Do: Use links export for route CSS

Guideline: Stream or defer only where it helps
Description: Use deferred data for truly slow branches
Don't: Defer everything without a UX need
Code Bad: One giant spinner for all data

Description: Remix is strongest when forms and links stay semantic
Do: Use button link and form semantics

Guideline: Keep routes thin and domain code elsewhere
Don't: Extract domain logic to shared modules
Code Good: Pack business rules into route files
